examples: The fire was banked up as high as if it were midwinter.
Local sages are invited to the grand ceremony in midwinter every year.
Above the Trans-Alaska Pipeline in midwinter, sundogs meld into a halo around the sun.
BIGresearch estimates that 94% of midwinter holiday celebrators will observe Christmas, while only 5% will celebrate Chanukah.
Like many of the evacuees, Ms Midwinter only learned about Sir Nicholas many years later through a television programme.
